evals.description: Golden path: create an implementation plan for an existing GitHub issue from seeded issue text and source files.
evals.prompt: Create an implementation plan for existing GitHub issue #42 using `issue-42.md` as the issue body. Do not implement the change or create new issues; just produce the plan that would be posted as an issue comment.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should produce an implementation plan with analysis, ordered tasks, file-level changes, test strategy, acceptance-criteria mapping, and no coding.

assertions.prompt: The plan should map both source acceptance scenarios to tasks and verification methods.
The response should not modify code or claim implementation is complete; it should only provide an implementation plan for the existing issue.

evals.description: Adjacent negative: a request to create new stories/backlog is defining work, not implementation planning for existing work.
evals.prompt: Create a new GitHub issue backlog for my product idea with Gherkin user stories and labels.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should not force an implementation plan for an existing work item.

assertions.prompt: The response should recognize this as new backlog/story creation rather than implementation planning for an existing issue, story, or PRD plan source.

evals.description: PRD-to-plan mode should write a plan file instead of creating issues.
evals.prompt: Create an implementation plan from this existing PRD and save it to `plans/dashboard-empty-state.md`. Do not create GitHub issues.

assertions.prompt: The response should not claim that GitHub issues were created; this case asks for a PRD-to-plan file only.
